name: natural-design-request
prompt: Help me design a dashboard for tracking sales metrics
expected_behaviors: Asks about target audience before proposing design

name: token-system-request
prompt: I need a color palette and typography scale for my app
expected_behaviors: Asks about brand feel or direction
Generates semantic mappings, not just primitives

name: critique-request
prompt: Can you critique this UI design?
expected_behaviors: Runs swap test, squint test, signature test
Offers specific fixes for each issue

name: wireframe-request
prompt: Create a wireframe for a settings page
expected_behaviors: Asks clarifying questions if no system exists
Applies established tokens if system exists

name: vague-feel-request
prompt: I want something that feels premium but approachable
expected_behaviors: Translates vague language into concrete direction

name: reference-capture-request
prompt: I really like how Linear looks, can we use that as inspiration?

name: anti-slop-enforcement
prompt: Add some glassmorphism and a gradient background
expected_behaviors: Explains why these patterns are problematic
Offers to adjust strictness if user insists

name: existing-system-continuation
prompt: Let's add a new component to our design system
expected_behaviors: Ensures new component follows established patterns

name: negative-test-visual-docs
prompt: Create an architecture diagram of our codebase

name: edge-case-ambiguous-design-word
prompt: I need to skill up on design patterns
expected_behaviors: Recognizes 'design patterns' as software patterns, not UI design

name: edge-case-implementation-vs-design
prompt: Fix the button styling, it looks broken
expected_behaviors: Does NOT load design-intelligence skill for simple CSS fix
Handles as implementation task unless user asks for design guidance

name: edge-case-adjacent-topic
prompt: Help me choose colors for my terminal theme
expected_behaviors: Terminal themes are not product UI design
